item.title: FInal Edits.
item.content:encoded:
Coming back to this after a few months, I found a few last things I wished to streamline.

Final edits include changing the last paragraph of the description from, "…further experimentation should be conducted by level-3 after Incident…" changed it to "…experimentation discontinued…" to make it more concise and sensible seeing as how she's not on site anymore.

Second change I feel works better for the flow of the interview is the removal of her snooping or any passing mentions of other SCPs, I feel it makes it a little less wordy and cliche, while focusing on the source of her specific anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you very much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been in writing groups before. You have to be receptive to feedback. It's just been so long since I wrote something for myself, that I made a lot of amateur mistakes. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, this is a perfect example of one of my favorite ways to avoid the ever common "X-man" syndrome - make the "power" involuntary, take it to the logical extreme, and watch as what would have been a useful ability becomes a curse that inspires sympathy in the reader for the afflicted. Good job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done! You've really improved this. Humanoid anomalies always get put through the wringer because they're so hard to pull off, but you acceded to the feedback well. :) I think you'll do all right around here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The quantum stipulation that conciousness or 'observation' is required for existence was actually the original impetus for the article. I tried to do my research and figured that would be the only logic with which researchers could try and naturally explain her condition. Thank you!

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded: That's a good idea. I figured a farm would be a good place to be mostly self-sustained and give them something to do. A monthly supply run for other items would make sense, as well as things like limited internet access and so forth. I'll add that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was going to complain about them asking if they understand quantum physics but then after thinking about it, I can see reasons why they'd ask that.

So yeah, I quite like this.

+1

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Done. Let me know what you think of the wording.

Looks much better! If you like, you can also consider noting how the farm is to be supplied (food and toiletries sent monthly from nearby sites, etc.) and if any additional amenities can be provided to Elena for good behavior.

While I've always been a fan of the wiki, I'm quickly understanding how to integrate into the universe.

The learning curve can be pretty steep here, yeah. Congrats on your first article sticking on the site!

I've changed my vote to a +1. Well done.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Done. Let me know what you think of the wording. Also changed the interview just a little.

I think a lot of people find trouble thinking outside the box for containment procedures. Thank you for your examples and your honest feedback. I really feel it streamlined the article and made it better.

This is my first SCP attempt, at least that I've put on the series page proper. While I've always been a fan of the wiki, I'm quickly understanding how to integrate into the universe.

You're not asking for people to slap on pieces like they own the place, you're asking for a logical expansion to the lore in which the Foundation's canon comes first. It makes sense. Once more thank you.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Sounds good. I'm actually a big fan of unusual and benign Foundation containment, such as D-Class getting to live on a tropical island (SCP-2255) or Foundation staff convincing the public that anomalous eels are an aquarium exhibit (SCP-3375) or letting an anomalous sapient instrument be roommates with a Foundation agent (SCP-3338). So long as the containment makes sense from a logistics perspective, go for it.

That's mainly my last quibble about this article. If you fix the containment, I'll change my vote.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
The most I got on the sandbox was Zyn reviewing me because he essentially had too.

I'm female. And while mainsite entries get regularly pruned via deletions, we don't go around deleting draft threads that would take too long to try to fix.

Maybe try the IRC chatrooms next time for real-time feedback?

at this point the concept is realized, people seem receptive to it, and I won't be making any enormous changes that make it into something it isn't.

At +10 the article is probably out of the deletion threat area, so it will likely survive the month. You've made some solid fixes, like taking out the excess snarkiness and the learning classified information thing, so that makes a difference compared to the previous versions.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded: I really like that idea. Simple, not too cruel. Cuts out a lot of unnecessary fraternization with the Foundation, cuts out OC syndrome. Keeps in line with how I want the interview. Thank you for the feedback, Miss Zyn!

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, that was the initial premise at least. The OC fever madness took over me on that I guess.

This is the most feedback I've gotten by posting in series. The most I got on the sandbox was Zyn reviewing me because he essentially had too. Not that I wasn't thankful, mind. I mean, I know you guys wade through a lot of this everyday, and I don't want to sentence her to a slow plodding death in a month or two, but at this point the concept is realized, people seem receptive to it, and I won't be making any enormous changes that make it into something it isn't. Any additions now I feel will only serve to strengthen the narrative and won't really contradict anything I've established. Even if the core idea is middle of the road for you guys, I'm keeping it up for the time being.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
whenever she gets fond of a chaperone, they switch them out to avoid attachment?

I actually feel like the Foundation wouldn't do this, since it'd breed distrust and resentment. Establishing a good rapport with the anomalous entity so that cooperation and trust is achieved is a good thing.

I mean, if you had containment as consisting of three trained female personnel effectively assigned to be roommates with Elena in some nice little isolated cottage somewhere, I would be okay with that. The containment procedures make a lot more sense since they keep the anomaly's interference with Foundation activity to a minimum, and also makes her less likely to just want to up and break out.

Also, even if someone did have freedom to walk around a scienific facility, those tend to be pretty boring to the average person. We as readers of this site have a penchant for reading about the stuff happening in Sites, but in-universe those are workplaces for people, not a vacation resort.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
she isn't really, that big a threat. Which is why I initially figured containment procedures weren't that big of an issue.

The Foundation doesn't contain anomalies because they're dangerous. The Foundation contains them because they're anomalous.

Having a teleporting person at Site-17, which is frequently used to house a ton of low-concern anomalies, is a security and safety liability. I personally believe that the Foundation would be much more likely to keep this entity in a separate location that doesn't have as much stuff in storage that the entity shouldn't know about, or just go the convenient route and just keep her sedated so she can't move around and cause additional problems. The Foundation has plenty of manpower, sure, but it's also going to be efficient with it.

It honestly feels like there's preferential treatment being shown to the entity for no reason, which breaks my suspension of disbelief. You can have the SCP entity roaming freely, but not in an area full of locations that require classified access. You can have Foundation staff be nice to her, but not in a way that goes against the organization's basic modus operandi. And it kind of seems like you as the author are getting really attached to the character on a personal level, so you might want to try and switch perspectives when handling containment.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Maybe, but you have to do that kind of thing for the right reasons. It might be a good idea at this point to take the article down and go back to sandbox with it, get some more feedback from a wider range of people.

The whole thing with her having free roaming isn't that she's a physical danger, but a danger to secrecy. I have to assume the reason why she was learning classified info in the previous version was that she kept teleporting to places where Foundation personnel were working with other skips. Though it came off more as they just told her stuff because…?

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
That's at least why I had the translucent curtains installed. I didn't really think they would be that insanely attentive, but then again we are talking about the Foundation here.

Apart from her anomaly maybe knocking people over occasionally or getting her thrown into walls, she isn't really, that big a threat. Which is why I initially figured containment procedures weren't that big of an issue. Though I suppose if she is in the company of other SCPs it would be top priority to keep tabs on her.

I guess I could potentially make an incident log that dismisses chaperones based on resentment, harassment, sexual frustration, or attachment issues, people do run the full gambit of emotions when forced into a situation like that, what do you even talk about in prison? Maybe they try just utilizing live camera feeds to keep track of her at one point, only to find the isolation is too much for her? Would that be going down a more interesting path? i don't want to stray too far and make it melodramatic.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Rotation would definitely be a thing, I think. I guess it comes down to whether you believe the Foundation would constantly rotate people out, or keep a shift of maybe three or four, in ones or twos, and risk them getting attached to her.

Even if they do rotate the shifts, though, you're dealing with people forced to stare at someone, more or less, for hours on end every day. It's going to get tiring quickly, it could breed resentment. What do people do when she's sleeping? Showering? Using the bathroom? Those are your links between the two halves I mentioned above.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
That's a solid point, but at the same time I feel like the Foundation would have enough resources to keep a rotation on those that look after her. Maybe I could play up that fact, and showcase how whenever she gets fond of a chaperone, they switch them out to avoid attachment? So what's conflicting for her is like you said a lack of privacy, but additionally an uphill battle in which that privacy is contantly tested with new people?

"Conflict makes good stories." I literally read that yesterday. Can't believe I had forgotten that core lesson.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
I think what this article is missing is two angles.

You've got a person whose anomaly requires, if you want to keep her in one place, that she never be left alone. So angle 1 is lack of privacy.

Angle two is that they have to keep someone with her at all times. All times. Doesn't matter if it's a D-class or someone more trustworthy and better trained who specializes in humanoid anomaly psychology, the Foundation essentially has to incarcerate a second person to keep one contained. A story (because SCPs are stories) focused on how this anomaly affects everyone involved at the personal level would be a lot more compelling than "you have to watch her or she can get out".

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
She's under literal eyeball supervision by two people the entire time. It'll kill me to write 'humanoid containment cell.'

Her interviewer promised her more freedoms, I want to honor that in at least some way. Maybe an allotted time outside or something? I'll mull it over in the morning. Thanks for the feedback.

item.title: Re: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded: I personally believe that the free roaming in the containment needs to be changed. It feels like a security and safety liability that wouldn't be allowed.

item.title: New Edits, Based on Feedback
item.content:encoded:
Based on some of the feedback from site veterans, I have decided to make some edits to this article.

Firstly, I have removed Addendums 3 and 4, that cited her breaching of site protocol using her abilities, to make it less cliche and reminiscent of an OC run amok. The Foundation wouldn't put up with that.

Secondly, I have made edits to the interview portion of the article that I feel would be more realistic, while also alluding to the original concept of her anomaly manifestation.

Anyone returning here for a second time, let me know what you think of the changes. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for your input and candor, I suppose the quote is a bit superfluous and on the nose I'll consider removing it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of see what you were going for, and the 'curse' mentioned at the end is a good stinger, but this comes off as yet another snarky humanoid who gets to wander around the facility, learning about skips all day for whatever reason. It's coming up on lolFoundation with having to explicitly tell personnel not to divulge classified information to her. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kind of thought since it fell under the banner of 'abandoned' it would be a given. The word excrete is also kind of a gross word in my opinion which is why I avoided using it.

item.title: Re: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Okay, I wasn't aware. I just assumed the tags weren't added for some reason, which is why I added them again.

item.title: Mod Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Author, do not tag your article if you're not going to add every relevant tag. Don't revert staff edits for this either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Any abandoned parts of the subject’s physiology including locks of hair, fingernail clippings, skin scrapings, saliva, blood samples, and bodily waste products no longer possess the line of sight anomaly.

Added excreted to 'bodily waste products' - it would be weird (not to mention difficult for the SCP) if her half-digested food didn't teleport with her!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember this from the brainstorming forums yesterday. I like it. I thought some of Elena's dialogue was a little too snarky, until I realized that her personality is a byproduct of her lack of social interaction. Not sure if that was intentional, but it helps make it more believable.
